videos.title: New to Major League Pickleball? Watch this first.
videos.description:
It’s not just pickleball, it’s team pickleball.
Coed lineups. City pride. Matches that come down to a final DreamBreaker™.

This is Major League Pickleball, where team chemistry is the difference maker and every event builds toward the hardest trophy to win in the sport. 

20 teams. The 2026 season takes place over 13 events this summer. Join us at an event or tune-in and catch your favorite team.

videos.title: 2026 MLP Draft presented by Skechers
videos.description:
Tune in starting at 10am ET as the 2026 MLP Draft presented by Skechers unfolds live. Dave Fleming and Will Daughton are breaking down the first 28 picks as the first two male and two female roster slots are filled for each team. Expect real-time reactions, insights, and a front row seat to understanding teams' selections.
⁠
The remaining 38 selections and complete rosters will be posted in real-t

videos.title: Inside MLP - 2026 Draft Rules and Preview
videos.description:
In this edition of Inside MLP, Will Daughton explains the new rules and parts of the 2026 MLP Draft, previews every team's pre-draft roster, and showcases the MLP Draft Show. All that and more on Inside MLP!

Stay connected and never miss an update!
👉 Live scoring, player stats, and event info: https://www.majorleaguepickleball.co
👇 Join our community on social!

videos.title: Inside MLP - 2026 Player Keeper Reactions
videos.description:
In this episode of Inside MLP, Dave Fleming and Will Daughton provide their initial reactions to the Player Keeper Deadline and discuss how some decisions could have massive implications for the upcoming Major League Pickleball draft on Feb. 27.

See the full list of Keepers by team here: https://majorleaguepickleball.co/news/major-league-pickleball-announces-2026-player-keepers/. 


Stay connecte

videos.title: What is Pickleball?
videos.description:
Pickleball is one of the fastest growing sports in the United States and for good reason. In this video, we break down what Pickleball is, why it’s so popular, and how you can get started playing.

Pickleball is a paddle sport that combines elements of tennis, table tennis, and badminton into one fun and accessible game. One of its biggest appeals is that players of all ages and skill levels can p

videos.title: Pickleball Fundamentals For Beginners
videos.description:
Having a strong foundation in pickleball fundamentals will set you up for long-term success on the court. In this video, we break down some of the most important fundamentals every beginner should learn to start playing with confidence.

First, it all starts with your grip. We recommend the continental grip, which you can find by forming a “V” with your dominant hand and placing it along the edge

videos.title: Most Important Rules to Start Playing Pickleball
videos.description:
If you don’t know the rules, you can’t play! In this video, we break down the most important pickleball rules so you can step on the court with confidence and start playing right away.

First, understand the boundaries of the court. The baselines and sidelines mark the limits, and for a ball to be in, it must land on or within these lines. If any part of the ball touches the line, it’s considered

videos.title: Volley Fundamentals in Pickleball
videos.description:
The volley is one of the most important and versatile shots in pickleball. In this video, we break down volley fundamentals so you can improve your control, reaction time, and ability to win points at the net.

A volley is any shot hit out of the air before the ball bounces. These shots can be offensive or defensive, hard or soft, but if you’re making contact before it hits the ground, it’s a voll

items.title: Tama Shimabukuro: Pickleball's newest superstar discusses Atlanta run that changed everything
items.summary: We wanted to learn more about Tama Shimabukuro, so earlier this week we invited him on PBallers, our remote version of Pickleballers. More

items.title: The Road to The Dink MiLP Nationals Runs Through These State Championships
items.summary: As teams across the country chase leaderboard points, Dream Tickets, and a shot at the 2027 Minor League Pickleball Championships, State Championships are quickly becoming some of the biggest stops on the calendar.
items.author: Luke Burton

items.title: 5 Pickleball Mistakes Keeping You Stuck Below 4.0
items.summary: Intermediate pickleball players often hit a plateau around the 3.0-4.0 level, not because of lack of effort, but because of repeated habits. A new breakdown identifies five specific pickleball mistakes that limit progression and explains exactly how to correct them.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The Pickleball Speedup: Pro Secrets to Instantly Win Points
items.summary: The pickleball speedup is one of the most misunderstood shots in the game, but it's also one of the most powerful when executed correctly. Pro player Ava Ignatowich breaks down exactly how to hit speedups, when to use them, and the triangle concept that separates amateurs from ad
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Mixed Doubles Strategy: Rachel Rohrabacher's Guide to Court Positioning
items.summary: Rachel Rohrabacher breaks down the fundamentals of mixed doubles strategy in a masterclass with Kyle Koszuta. From understanding each player's role to reading your opponent's movement, this guide covers everything you need to elevate your mixed game.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Understanding Pickleball Stacking Strategies
items.summary: Pickleball stacking is a game-changing doubles strategy that lets you position your forehands in the middle of the court. Whether you're playing mixed doubles or teaming with a lefty, understanding both off-court and on-court stacking methods will transform your competitive edge.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Why Your Pickleball Serve Feels Weak and How to Fix It
items.summary: Your pickleball serve doesn't have to feel weak and unreliable. Coach Jess breaks down three simple fixes that transform your pickleball serve in minutes, from toss consistency to proper weight transfer.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Hit an ATP in Pickleball: Around-the-Post Step by Step
items.summary: The ATP around the post in pickleball is one of the most spectacular shots in the game, and it's totally legal. This guide breaks down the setup, mechanics, and decision-making to help you execute it in a real match.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Ranking the Best Women's Pickleball Shoes of 2026
items.summary: I tried the 9 top women's pickleball shoes from Nike, ASICS, Selkirk, JOOLA and more to reveal the best options for comfort, speed, and on-court performance.
items.author: Emily Visnic

items.title: Master the Kitchen Dink: Pro Tips for Better Pickleball Play
items.summary: The kitchen dink is the foundation of modern pickleball strategy. Master depth control, target placement, and counter-anticipation to dominate at the net.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Mental Game for Competitive Pickleball: Stay Calm Under Pressure and Win
items.summary: The mental game competitive pickleball demands is just as important as your backhand or your third shot drop. Learn how top players stay calm under pressure, reset after errors, and build the focus that wins matches.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: 2026 PPA Tour Finals: Format, pools and schedule

items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: Why Your Pickleball Return of Serve Might Be More Important Than Your Serve
items.summary: Most pickleball players obsess over their serve, but the truth is your pickleball return of serve might be even more important. Here's why depth, placement, and consistency matter more than power.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Win Dinking Rallies: Patience, Placement and Setting Up the Attack
items.summary: Knowing how to win dinking rallies in pickleball separates reactive players from players who actually control the point. This guide breaks down the placement targets, patience principles, and attack triggers that make dinking a genuine weapon.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: 5 Pickleball Serves That Win More Points Instantly
items.summary: Adding even one of these serves into your game can transform your effectiveness instantly.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Off-Ball Positioning in Pickleball: How to Master Net Control
items.summary: Most pickleball players think the point is decided by whoever hits the ball, but at higher levels, off-ball positioning is what actually controls the court. Master this skill and you'll transform how you play at the net.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Attack the Triangle Pattern in Pickleball Doubles
items.summary: The pickleball triangle pattern is a doubles strategy where you attack one player’s jam zone, then anticipate the likely counter lane. Instead of speeding up randomly, you target the inside hip, shoulder, chicken wing, or lower body to create a predictable next ball for you or yo
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Turn Your Punch Volley Into a Finish Shot
items.summary: To turn a pickleball punch volley into a winner, take the ball early, keep your wrist stable, stay compact, and move the paddle forward through contact. Aim at the opponent’s feet, hip, middle seam, or open space instead of swinging harder. The goal is controlled pressure, not a
items.author: Ana

items.title: Can Watching Pro Pickleball Actually Make You Better?
items.summary: Watching pro pickleball can improve your game if you watch with a purpose. Instead of copying highlight shots, study footwork, positioning, shot selection, recovery, patience, and how pros build points. Pick one player, one skill, and one habit to apply in your next rec game. Yes
items.author: Ana

items.title: I watched every episode of the new pro pickleball docuseries 'Partners' -- here's what I learned

items.title: How to help families of plane crash victims who were heading to pickleball tournament
items.summary: Fundraisers and meal trains have been set up for the families of the five people who were killed in a Texas plane crash on their way to a pickleball tournament last week. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: What to Do When Nobody Dinks in Rec Pickleball
items.summary: When nobody dinks in rec pickleball, don’t force soft rallies. Use deep returns, blocks, resets, low middle balls, crosscourt dinks, and smart counters to make hard attacks less comfortable. With non-dinking partners, keep cues simple and focus on one controllable soft ball at a
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Read Where Your Opponent Will Hit the Ball in Pickleball
items.summary: To read where your opponent will hit in pickleball, don’t just watch the paddle face. Read the contact window, shoulders, feet, balance, court geometry, and likely counter lanes. These cues help you narrow the possible shots earlier, react faster, and stop guessing during rallies
items.author: Ana

items.title: If You Play Less Pickleball, Will You Get Worse?
items.summary: Playing less pickleball will not automatically make you worse. You may lose some sharpness at first, but you can maintain and still improve with focused sessions, smart drilling, competitive games, strength work, recovery, and short touch primers before play. Fewer sessions need
items.author: Ana

items.author: Drew McCartt

items.title: How to Hit a Pickleball Tweener Without Falling Over
items.summary: A pickleball tweener is a last-resort shot used when a lob gets behind you. Let the ball drop to knee height, swing upward between your legs, and keep moving for balance. Focus on getting it back in play—usually as a soft lob—rather than hitting a winner. Let’s clear this up righ
items.author: Ana

items.title: How PodPlay is helping pickleball facilities grow their business
items.summary: PodPlay is a comprehensive management system that allows facility operators to handle court bookings, payments, leagues and events all in one place. More

items.title: The Best Pickleball Socks of 2026: Comfort, Grip & Style That Actually Matter
items.summary: The best pickleball socks offer targeted cushioning, moisture control, arch support, and a secure fit for quick lateral movement. Top options include Thorlos, Selkirk, Balega, ChalkTalkSPORTS, and Diadem, depending on whether you want maximum comfort, pickleball-specific performa
items.author: Ana

items.title: Roll It or Smack It? The Contact-Point Rule Intermediate Pickleball Players Keep Missing
items.summary: Most intermediate rec players do not lose attacking points because they are afraid to be aggressive. They lose them because they attack the wrong ball with the wrong swing. They try to roll a ball that is already high enough to punish. Or they try to smack a ball that is too low
items.author: Ana
